design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate nonlinear levels, eliminate demon hordes using varied weapons, find exit to progress. Exploration rewards hidden bonuses.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammo, health) against escalating demon density in confined spaces. Nonlinear design creates navigation uncertainty.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone marine fighting overwhelming demonic forces. Empowerment through weapon acquisition and survival against increasingly elaborate monster encounters.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetically arcade-focused: no narrative scaffolding, minimal story interruption, pure combat as content. Levels are intricate spatial puzzles rewarding exploration over linear progression. Refuses to hold player hand—nonlinear design assumes player agency in route-finding.

difficulty.lives_system: Health bar system; player death results in level restart. No lives pool in traditional sense.
difficulty.continues_system: Level restart on death; no continue/checkpoint within level documented
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level checkpoints only. Completion of level preserves inventory for next level.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty selection available (inferred from Doom legacy). Nonlinear level design allows players to sequence encounters—can skip optional areas or detour for health/ammo pickups before engaging harder combat zones.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup system (8+ weapons including new Super Shotgun). Power-ups like Megasphere and health packs found in levels. No RPG-style stat upgrades.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Secret levels accessible from level 15. New enemy types introduced across 32 levels to maintain escalation. No gate-based unlocks; progression is episode-sequential.
replayability_hooks: Nonlinear level design encourages alternate routes
Master Levels expansion pack with 21 additional levels

world_building_techniques: Interludes between level clusters advance plot minimally
Story stated as 'immediately following events of Doom' but not elaborated in-game
Levels present demon-infested Earth; Hell architecture as visual theme

boss_design.philosophy: Boss monsters from Doom repurposed as regular enemies in Doom II, indicating hierarchy flattening. New bosses introduced but exact mechanics not detailed in sources. Megademon (Cyberdemon) visible on cover art.
notable_bosses: Other original Doom bosses recontextualized as standard enemies

faction_indicators: Demons/Hell forces vs. Human marine (implicit duality)
No faction system; binary enemy/player dynamic
danger_signals: Monster density increases with progression across 32 levels
Larger, more intricate levels create spatial disorientation threat
Resource scarcity (ammo depletion) signals danger escalation
Nonlinear design traps: players can wander into overpowered encounters
environmental_storytelling: Hell-themed architecture signals demonic invasion of Earth
Weapon and health distribution guides safe vs. dangerous zones
Level layout implies narrative progression (Earth to Hell descent implied)
Interlude screens show background changes reflecting story beats

sources_used: wikipedia - Doom II main article (gameplay section, plot intro, releases, development credits)
